requirements.original_text: The Station shall be designed, manufactured, type-tested, delivered, erected, commissioned, and put into operation as a complete 525 kV / 2 GW HVDC converter station, suitable for the onshore end of the NordLink-3 offshore wind connection.
requirements.ears_normalized: The Station shall be supplied as a complete 525 kV / 2 GW HVDC converter station — designed, manufactured, type-tested, delivered, erected, commissioned, and operational — for the onshore end of the NordLink-3 connection.
requirements.constraint: 525 kV symmetrical monopolar, 2 GW, onshore terminal

requirements.original_text: The Station shall be supplied with all hardware, software, primary and secondary equipment, civil works, documentation, type-test evidence, and integration services required to satisfy the requirements of this document.
requirements.ears_normalized: The Station shall be supplied complete with all primary and secondary equipment, civil works, documentation, type-test evidence, and integration services required by this document.
requirements.constraint: primary + secondary + civils + documentation + type tests + integration

requirements.original_text: The Contractor shall maintain the Station under a service agreement of no less than five (5) years from Provisional Acceptance.
requirements.ears_normalized: The Contractor shall maintain the Station under a service agreement for at least five (5) years from Provisional Acceptance.

requirements.original_text: The Station shall connect to the TSO's 380 kV AC busbar at the Point of Common Coupling, in compliance with the TSO's grid connection conditions and with Commission Regulation (EU) 2016/1447 (Network Code on Requirements for Generators and HVDC Systems).
requirements.ears_normalized: The Station shall connect to the TSO's 380 kV AC busbar at the PCC, complying with the TSO's grid connection conditions and Commission Regulation (EU) 2016/1447.
requirements.action: connect to TSO 380 kV AC busbar at PCC
requirements.constraint: comply with TSO grid code + EU 2016/1447

requirements.original_text: The DC side of the Station shall operate at a rated symmetrical monopolar voltage of ±525 kV with a continuous transmission capacity of 2 GW per pole.
requirements.ears_normalized: The DC side of the Station shall operate at ±525 kV symmetrical monopolar with continuous transmission capacity of 2 GW per pole.
requirements.action: operate DC side at rated voltage and capacity
requirements.constraint: ±525 kV symmetrical monopolar; 2 GW continuous per pole; per Annex C.2, also up to 2.2 GW per pole for ≤ 30 min for redispatch

requirements.original_text: The Station shall operate as a Modular Multilevel Converter (MMC) of half-bridge or full-bridge topology, at the Contractor's choice, subject to type-test evidence for the chosen topology being available at the design phase.
requirements.ears_normalized: The Station shall operate as a Modular Multilevel Converter (half-bridge or full-bridge, Contractor's choice), provided type-test evidence for the chosen topology is available at design phase.
requirements.trigger_condition: design phase requires demonstrable type-test evidence
requirements.action: operate as MMC of chosen topology
requirements.constraint: half-bridge or full-bridge; type-test evidence required

requirements.original_text: When the DC voltage at the converter terminals deviates from nominal by more than ±5%, the Station's control shall regulate the converter to restore nominal voltage within the response times defined in the TSO's grid connection conditions.
requirements.ears_normalized: When the DC voltage at the converter terminals deviates from nominal by more than ±5%, the Station's control shall regulate the converter to restore nominal voltage within the TSO's grid-code response times.
requirements.trigger_condition: DC voltage at the converter terminals deviates from nominal by more than ±5%
requirements.action: regulate converter to restore nominal DC voltage

requirements.original_text: The Station shall provide reactive power support at the PCC within the operating envelope of −0.95 to +0.95 cos φ at rated active power.
requirements.ears_normalized: The Station shall provide reactive power support at the PCC within an operating envelope of −0.95 to +0.95 cos φ at rated active power (per Annex C.4, this requirement now includes continuous FRT for a 250 ms zero-voltage AC fault at the PCC).
requirements.action: provide reactive power support at PCC
requirements.constraint: −0.95 to +0.95 cos φ at rated active power; Annex C.4 adds zero-voltage FRT for ≤ 250 ms

requirements.original_text: The Station shall achieve a measured technical availability of at least 99.0% over any rolling twelve-month period, excluding scheduled outage windows agreed in writing with the TSO.
requirements.ears_normalized: The Station shall achieve measured technical availability of at least 99.0% over any rolling twelve-month period, excluding scheduled outages.
requirements.constraint: over any rolling 12 months, excluding scheduled outages

requirements.original_text: The Station's converter shall achieve an energy efficiency at rated active power of no less than 98.7% per converter end (AC-to-DC or DC-to-AC, as applicable), measured at the connection terminals.
requirements.ears_normalized: The converter shall achieve energy efficiency ≥ 98.7% per converter end at rated active power, measured at the connection terminals.

requirements.original_text: While a planned outage window is in effect, the Station may operate at reduced active power provided that DC voltage stability and AC-side reactive support remain compliant with §3.4 and §3.5.
requirements.ears_normalized: While a planned outage window is in effect, the Station may operate at reduced active power provided that DC voltage stability and reactive support remain compliant with §3.4 and §3.5.
requirements.trigger_condition: a planned outage window is in effect
requirements.constraint: DC voltage stability + reactive support must remain compliant

requirements.original_text: If a single converter submodule fails, the Station shall continue operation by activating its installed redundancy, without operator intervention and without a step change in transmitted active power.
requirements.ears_normalized: If a single converter submodule fails, the Station shall continue operation by activating installed redundancy, without operator intervention and without a step change in transmitted active power.
requirements.action: continue operation by activating installed redundancy
requirements.constraint: no operator intervention; no step change in active power

requirements.original_text: The Station shall recover from a single full-pole trip and resume power transmission to the agreed operating point within the times defined in the TSO's grid connection conditions.
requirements.ears_normalized: After a single full-pole trip, the Station shall recover and resume transmission to the agreed operating point within the TSO's grid-code recovery times.

requirements.original_text: The Station's protection and control system shall be implemented in accordance with IEC 61850 for all internal substation communications.
requirements.ears_normalized: The Station's protection and control system shall implement IEC 61850 for all internal substation communications.

requirements.original_text: The Station's AC switchgear shall comply with IEC 62271-1 and the applicable part-standards (IEC 62271-100 for circuit breakers, IEC 62271-102 for disconnectors and earthing switches, IEC 62271-203 for gas-insulated metal-enclosed switchgear).
requirements.ears_normalized: The Station's AC switchgear shall comply with IEC 62271-1 and the applicable part-standards (-100 circuit breakers, -102 disconnectors / earthing switches, -203 GIS).

requirements.original_text: The Station shall provide independent main protection (Main 1 and Main 2) for each protected zone, with no single point of common failure between the two main systems.
requirements.ears_normalized: The Station shall provide independent Main 1 / Main 2 protection for each protected zone, with no single point of common failure between the two mains.
requirements.action: provide independent Main 1/Main 2 protection per zone
requirements.constraint: no single point of common failure

requirements.original_text: The Station's protection and control system shall comply with IEC 62443-3-3 controls SR 1.1 through SR 1.5, SR 2.1, SR 3.1, SR 3.4, and SR 7.6, applied at the system-integrator level of the Contractor's delivery organisation.
requirements.ears_normalized: The Station's protection-and-control system shall comply with IEC 62443-3-3 controls SR 1.1–SR 1.5, SR 2.1, SR 3.1, SR 3.4, and SR 7.6, at the Contractor system-integrator level.
requirements.ambiguity_notes: Requirement is incorporated by reference to IEC 62443-3-3. Each cited control carries its own sub-requirements; a literal reading expands this single 'shall' into ~25 obligations on the Contractor system-integrator organisation. Clarify with the TSO whether certified compliance at a stated Security Level is acceptable, or per-control evidence is required.

requirements.original_text: All disturbance and event records shall be tamper-evident and retained for no less than seven (7) years.
requirements.ears_normalized: All disturbance and event records shall be tamper-evident and retained for at least seven (7) years.
requirements.action: retain disturbance and event records, tamper-evident

requirements.original_text: The Station shall include converter transformers conforming to IEC 60076-57-129 (Power transformers — Part 57-129: Transformers for HVDC applications). The rated MVA and short-circuit impedance shall be proposed by the Contractor based on the converter design.
requirements.ears_normalized: The Station shall include converter transformers conforming to IEC 60076-57-129; rated MVA and short-circuit impedance are Contractor-proposed.

requirements.original_text: The Station shall provide harmonic filtering and reactive compensation such that the harmonic distortion at the PCC remains within the limits defined in the TSO's grid connection conditions and IEEE Std 519-2022. Filter design shall be of either passive or active type at the Contractor's choice.
requirements.ears_normalized: The Station shall provide harmonic filtering and reactive compensation such that PCC harmonic distortion remains within the TSO grid-code limits and IEEE Std 519-2022. Filter type (passive or active) is Contractor's choice.
requirements.action: provide harmonic filtering and reactive compensation
requirements.constraint: PCC distortion within TSO + IEEE 519-2022 limits; passive or active

requirements.original_text: The Station shall protect personal data in accordance with applicable data-protection regulations. The Contractor shall name the regulations in the response.
requirements.ears_normalized: The Station shall protect personal data per applicable data-protection regulations; the Contractor identifies the regulations in the response.

requirements.original_text: On TSO request the Station shall delete personal data within thirty (30) days. Where retention is required by law, the Station shall retain only the minimum necessary data and shall record the legal basis.
requirements.ears_normalized: When the TSO requests deletion of personal data, the Station shall delete it within thirty (30) days, except where law mandates retention, in which case minimum-necessary data is retained with the legal basis recorded.
requirements.trigger_condition: the TSO requests deletion of personal data
requirements.constraint: within 30 days; minimum-necessary retention only when legally required

requirements.original_text: When the TSO control centre issues a setpoint change via the station's gateway, the Station shall log the setpoint, the issuing operator identity, the timestamp, and the previous setpoint before applying the change to the converter control.
requirements.ears_normalized: When the TSO control centre issues a setpoint change via the gateway, the Station shall log the new setpoint, operator identity, timestamp, and previous setpoint before applying the change.
requirements.trigger_condition: the TSO control centre issues a setpoint change via the gateway
requirements.action: log setpoint change and metadata before application
requirements.constraint: new + previous setpoint, operator identity, timestamp; log written before apply

requirements.original_text: When a Priority 1 alarm is raised, the Station shall present the alarm in the operator HMI within 500 milliseconds of the underlying event being detected.
requirements.ears_normalized: When any alarm (not only Priority 1) is raised, the Station shall present it in the operator HMI within 250 milliseconds of the underlying event being detected (per Annex C.1, which supersedes the original 500 ms / P1-only limit).
requirements.trigger_condition: any alarm is raised (per Annex C.1, not only Priority 1)
requirements.constraint: within 250 ms (Annex C.1 modifies §7.2: 500 ms → 250 ms, all priorities)

requirements.original_text: If two operators issue conflicting setpoints to the same converter pole within one (1) second, the Station shall reject both commands and raise an alarm.
requirements.ears_normalized: If two operators issue conflicting setpoints to the same converter pole within one (1) second, the Station shall reject both and raise an alarm.
requirements.trigger_condition: two operators issue conflicting setpoints to the same pole within 1 s
requirements.action: reject both setpoints and raise alarm

requirements.original_text: The Station shall present the operational state of every primary asset on a single overview screen suitable for control-room display.
requirements.ears_normalized: The Station shall present the operational state of every primary asset on a single control-room overview screen.
requirements.action: present primary-asset state on overview HMI

requirements.original_text: The Station shall produce a daily operations report summarising transmitted energy, availability metrics, alarm counts by priority, and any protection operations.
requirements.ears_normalized: The Station shall produce a daily operations report covering transmitted energy, availability metrics, alarm counts by priority, and protection operations.

requirements.original_text: The Station shall produce a monthly grid-code compliance report listing all relevant events affecting compliance with the TSO's grid connection conditions. The report format shall be agreed during design.
requirements.ears_normalized: The Station shall produce a monthly grid-code compliance report listing relevant events; format agreed during design.

requirements.original_text: Where requested by the TSO, the Station shall produce ad-hoc disturbance reports covering up to ninety (90) days of records within sixty (60) minutes of request.
requirements.ears_normalized: Where the TSO requests an ad-hoc disturbance report, the Station shall produce one covering up to ninety (90) days of records within sixty (60) minutes.
requirements.trigger_condition: the TSO requests an ad-hoc disturbance report
requirements.constraint: ≤ 90 days of records; ≤ 60 minutes to produce

requirements.original_text: The Contractor shall provide adequate reactive-power support for the Station.
requirements.ears_normalized: The Contractor shall provide adequate reactive-power support for the Station.
requirements.ambiguity_notes: The qualifier 'adequate' carries no measurable acceptance criterion, and §9.1's text is in tension with §3.5 (which sets a quantitative cos φ envelope) and Annex C.4 (which adds FRT). Clarify the intended scope and measure with the TSO before any commitment — likely an editorial leftover from Revision A.

requirements.original_text: The Station shall be capable of black start support to the local auxiliary network in the event of a wide-area blackout, with a documented energisation sequence proposed by the Contractor.
requirements.ears_normalized: If a wide-area blackout occurs, the Station shall be capable of black-start support to the local auxiliary network per a Contractor-proposed energisation sequence.
requirements.action: provide black-start support to local auxiliary network

requirements.original_text: The Station shall not lose accepted setpoint history in the event of a single-cubicle protection-and-control failure.
requirements.ears_normalized: If a single protection-and-control cubicle fails, the Station shall not lose any accepted setpoint history.

requirements.original_text: The Contractor shall provide operations, maintenance, and protection documentation in German and English at handover.
requirements.ears_normalized: The Contractor shall provide operations, maintenance, and protection documentation in German and English at handover.

requirements.original_text: The Contractor shall provide initial training for up to twelve (12) control-room operators and four (4) protection engineers at the TSO's premises.
requirements.ears_normalized: The Contractor shall provide initial training for up to twelve (12) control-room operators and four (4) protection engineers at the TSO's premises.
requirements.constraint: up to 12 operators + 4 protection engineers; on TSO premises

requirements.original_text: The Contractor shall provide refresher training annually for the duration of the service agreement defined in §2.3.
requirements.ears_normalized: The Contractor shall provide refresher training annually for the duration of the §2.3 service agreement.

requirements.original_text: The Contractor shall provide a firmware-update channel for the Station's protection and control system that does not require connectivity from the production network to the public internet.
requirements.ears_normalized: The Contractor shall provide a firmware-update channel for the protection-and-control system that does not require connectivity from the production network to the public internet.

requirements.original_text: Where a third-party component is no longer supported by its vendor, the Contractor shall propose a replacement at least twelve (12) months before end-of-support.
requirements.ears_normalized: Where a third-party component reaches end-of-support with its vendor, the Contractor shall propose a replacement at least twelve (12) months in advance.

requirements.original_text: The Contractor shall provide 24x7 incident response with a Priority 1 acknowledgment time of no more than fifteen (15) minutes.
requirements.ears_normalized: The Contractor shall provide 24x7 incident response with Priority 1 acknowledgment within fifteen (15) minutes during TSO operating hours (06:00–22:00 business days), and within thirty (30) minutes outside those hours (per Annex C.3).
requirements.constraint: P1 ack ≤ 15 min in TSO operating hours; ≤ 30 min outside (Annex C.3 clarifies §11.3)

requirements.original_text: The Contractor shall provide a fixed price for the Station at handover, broken down by primary equipment, secondary equipment, civil works, integration, and services.
requirements.ears_normalized: The Contractor shall provide a fixed price at handover, broken down by primary equipment, secondary equipment, civil works, integration, and services.

requirements.original_text: The Contractor shall provide unit prices for additional bay capacity (per AC switchgear bay and per DC reactor unit).
requirements.ears_normalized: The Contractor shall provide unit prices for additional bay capacity, per AC switchgear bay and per DC reactor unit.
requirements.constraint: per AC switchgear bay; per DC reactor unit

context_facts.text: TSO operating hours per Annex C.3: 06:00–22:00 local time on TSO business days, unless stated otherwise.
Document is Revision B (issued 2026-03-14), superseding Revision A of 2026-02-01.
Annex C clarifications were issued with Revision B in response to the bidders' clarification meeting of 2026-02-22. Where Annex C modifies §1–§12, Annex C prevails.
PCC = Point of Common Coupling — the connection point to the 380 kV AC grid; HVDC link = full bipolar 525 kV DC link from offshore platform to onshore PCC.

commercial_terms.text: Failure to comply with a 'shall' requirement is a non-compliance.
'Should' requirements may be non-complied with provided the response justifies the non-compliance.
Failure to demonstrate continuous fault-ride-through capability per Annex C.4 results in a non-compliance and Liquidated Damages per the contract schedule.

contradictions.reason: §7.2 in the main body states a 500 ms presentation limit for Priority 1 alarms only; Annex C.1 supersedes this to a 250 ms limit applying to all alarm priorities. The normalised requirement reflects Annex C.1.

§3.2 states a continuous 2 GW per pole; Annex C.2 clarifies that the Station shall additionally be capable of 2.2 GW per pole continuously for periods of up to 30 minutes for TSO redispatch. The normalised requirement reflects both.

§3.5 specifies the reactive-power operating envelope; Annex C.4 modifies the same clause to add continuous fault-ride-through capability for a 250 ms zero-voltage AC fault at the PCC, with Liquidated Damages on failure. The normalised requirement reflects both.
§11.3 states a 15-minute P1 acknowledgment time; Annex C.3 clarifies this applies during TSO operating hours only, with a 30-minute target outside. The normalised requirement reflects Annex C.3.

gaps.explanation: §4.4 requires the Station to ride through a single submodule failure with installed redundancy, but the document is silent on the minimum redundancy level (e.g. N+5, N+8) the Contractor must offer. Suggest raising as a clarification — common industry practice is N+8 or higher per arm.

§6.3 requires compliance with 'applicable data-protection regulations' without naming them. The Contractor should name them in the response (GDPR for the EU operating environment) and have the TSO confirm.
The document is silent on the station-earthing standard and the touch-/step-voltage acceptance criteria. EN 50522 is the European reference; clarify whether it is the contractual standard.

quality_analysis.executive_summary:
## Executive Summary

TSO-HVDC-2026-0142 (Rev. B) contains **40 requirements** across thirteen functional sections plus Annex C, covering the onshore 525 kV / 2 GW HVDC converter station of the NordLink-3 offshore wind connection. The document is well-structured and grid-code aware, but carries three patterns that need engineering attention before the Contractor responds:

1. **Annex C overrides (four requirements affected)** — REQ-005 (§3.2: continuous 2 GW + 30-min 2.2 GW for redispatch), REQ-008 (§3.5: reactive envelope + 250 ms zero-voltage FRT with Liquidated Damages), REQ-024 (§7.2 alarm latency: 500 ms P1-only → 250 ms all alarms), and REQ-038 (§11.3 incident response: 15 min in operating hours / 30 min outside). The normalised requirements above already reflect Annex C; the Contractor should confirm the reading in writing with the TSO.
2. **Standard incorporated by reference (REQ-017)** — a single 'shall' invokes ten specific IEC 62443-3-3 system requirements at the system-integrator level. Each control carries its own sub-obligations; clarify whether certified compliance at a stated Security Level (SL-T) is accepted, or per-control evidence is required.
3. **Unmeasurable wording (REQ-030)** — §9.1 'shall provide adequate reactive-power support' has no measurable criterion and is in tension with the quantitative §3.5 / Annex C.4 envelope. Likely an editorial leftover from Revision A; clarify with the TSO before any commitment.

Three coverage gaps were also identified (submodule redundancy level, regulatory-scope naming for personal data, earthing-system standard). All are best raised as clarifications before the response is finalised.

Of the 40 requirements, **nine carry penalty / Liquidated-Damages language** — most notably grid-code compliance at the PCC (REQ-004), availability (REQ-009), submodule-failure ride-through (REQ-012), zero-voltage FRT (REQ-008 via Annex C.4), alarm-latency (REQ-024 via Annex C.1), single-cubicle loss-of-history (REQ-032), and incident-response (REQ-038). These deserve special attention in the compliance matrix.
